nodes.x: Furthermore Elihu answered and said,
Elihu also proceeded, and said,
Elihu spake moreover, and said,
Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,
Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.
Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
And again, verily I say unto you, to some is given, by the Spirit of God, the word of wisdom.
A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:
For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.
Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?
Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is clovenfooted, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.
The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.
Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.
Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.

judge ourselves. In reference to our own acts and feelings, we are liable to judge prematurely and to decide favourably; but in that judgment let us use the same calm wisdom and firm decision that we should use in judging others.

_Judgment According to Works_--This is a subject I have reflected
upon a great deal, and I have come to the conclusion that we shall be
judged according to the deeds done in the body and according to the
thoughts and intents of the heart. 14:99.
For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
The Lord shall judge the people: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.

For behold, my brethren, it is given unto you to judge, that ye may know good from evil; and the way to judge is as plain, that ye may know with a perfect knowledge, as the daylight is from the dark night.
For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.
Hath walked in my statutes, and hath kept my judgments, to deal truly; he is just, he shall surely live, saith the Lord God.
As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;

And he hath confirmed his words, which he spake against us, and against our judges that judged us, by bringing upon us a great evil: for under the whole heaven hath not been done as hath been done upon Jerusalem.
Therefore the Lord hath recompensed me according to my righteousness; according to my cleanness in his eye sight.
But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.
Why is my pain perpetual, and my wound incurable, which refuseth to be healed? wilt thou be altogether unto me as a liar, and as waters that fail?
¶ Woe is me for my hurt! my wound is grievous: but I said, Truly this is a grief, and I must bear it.
For thus saith the Lord, Thy bruise is incurable, and thy wound is grievous.
My wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness.
What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?
How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?
That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,

nodes.lb: GC 2003/04 — Living Water By Kathleen H. Hughes
nodes.x: Blessed by Living Water By Kathleen H. Hughes First Counselor in the Relief Society General Presidency Living water heals. It nourishes and sustains. It brings peace and joy. Early in Christ’s ministry, He traveled from Jerusalem to His boyhood home of Nazareth, in Galilee. He passed through Samaria and, weary from His journey, stopped to rest at Jacob’s ancient well. As Jesus waited and His…
He turneth the wilderness into a standing water, and dry ground into watersprings.
Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.
Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, which speak peace to their neighbours, but mischief is in their hearts.
Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:
When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity do flourish; it is that they shall be destroyed for ever:
The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.
Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.
But if he thus say, I have no delight in thee; behold, here am I, let him do to me as seemeth good unto him.
For behold, God hath said a man being evil cannot do that which is good; for if he offereth a gift, or prayeth unto God, except he shall do it with real intent it profiteth him nothing.
Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?
For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
But his delight is in the law of the Lord; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.

sir,’ said I, “are the wickednesses from which we 
must refrain?” “ Listen,’ said he. “ From adultery 
and fornication, from the lawlessness of drunkenness, 
from evil luxury, from much eating, and extravagance 
of wealth, and boastfulness and haughtiness and 
pride, and from lying and evil speaking and 
hypocrisy, malice and all blasphemy. 4. These 
deeds are the wickedest of all in the life of…
And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
And behold, if the Lord shall say unto a man—Because of thine iniquities, thou shalt be accursed forever—it shall be done.
For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.
Who will render to every man according to his deeds:
Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
Every man’s work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man’s work of what sort it is.
And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.
Unto the day when the Lord shall come to recompense unto every man according to his work, and measure to every man according to the measure which he has measured to his fellow man.
Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.
Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?
But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake the wicked; and it is by the wicked that the wicked are punished; for it is the wicked that stir up the hearts of the children of men unto bloodshed.
The Lord preserveth all them that love him: but all the wicked will he destroy.

not God are to perish because of their wickedness, how much more will he visit those who have greater light and knowledge if they will not keep his commandments? The Lord sent forth the destroyer in ancient times to lay waste the firstborn of the Egyptians, pointing out the means by which his people might escape, and those who failed to do as they were commanded had no promise of being preserved;…
For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.
Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?
He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his discretion.
And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.

And it came to pass that according to his word he did destroy them; and according to his word he did lead them; and according to his word he did do all things for them; and there was not any thing done save it were by his word.
If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
And shall consume the glory of his forest, and of his fruitful field, both soul and body; and they shall be as when a standard-bearer fainteth.
A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.

So also the attributes of the spirit of man--the attributes of the
mind--now imperfect, impure, unholy, and limited in the range of vision
and apprehension of things, owing largely to the conditions in which
man finds himself placed in this earth-life (and all for a wise purpose
in God's economy); yet the time will come that it will be with the
spirit as with the body; for God shall change our…
All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.
All go unto one place; all are of the dust, and all turn to dust again.
Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.
They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.
If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,
Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
Nevertheless hear thou now this word that I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people;
How is it that ye have not hearkened unto the word of the Lord?
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?

Yet I am sorry that he is not a little more magnanimous; I am sorry he does not possess a little more of these feelings that actuated the founders of this government; I am not sorry for the Saints, for it is quite necessary that we should have to pass through a variety of things in order that, like ancient Saints, we may be made perfect through suffering. “For it became him, for whom are all…
For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.
And now, O king, what great evil hast thou done, or what great sins have thy people committed, that we should be condemned of God or judged of this man?
Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked? and to princes, Ye are ungodly?
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
But if ye shall still do wickedly, ye shall be consumed, both ye and your king.

But if the princes hear that I have talked with thee, and they come unto thee, and say unto thee, Declare unto us now what thou hast said unto the king, hide it not from us, and we will not put thee to death; also what the king said unto thee:
He shall cut off the spirit of princes: he is terrible to the kings of the earth.
How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all are the work of his hands.
That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.
In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.

And now, when I the Lord bad spoken these words unto my diaeiples, they ware troubled ; and I said unto them, be not troubled, for when all these things shall coma to pftft,yo may know that the promises which have been made unto yeu, shall be fulfilled : and when the light shall begin to bteak forth, it shall be with them like ante a parable which I will show you: ye look and behold the…
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
For the ways of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.
For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.
There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
For behold, my beloved brethren, I say unto you that the Lord God worketh not in darkness.

There is nothing which is secret save it shall be revealed; there is no work of darkness save it shall be made manifest in the light; and there is nothing which is sealed upon the earth save it shall be loosed.
The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light; they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.
The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light: they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.
A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.
For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.
He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
And he shall rise the third day from the dead; and behold, he standeth to judge the world; and behold, all these things are done that a righteous judgment might come upon the children of men.
And enter not into judgment with thy servant: for in thy sight shall no man living be justified.
For the Lord will judge his people, and he will repent himself concerning his servants.
And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.
They shall run like mighty men; they shall climb the wall like men of war; and they shall march every one on his ways, and they shall not break their ranks:

And he will appoint him captains over thousands, and captains over fifties; and will set them to ear his ground, and to reap his harvest, and to make his instruments of war, and instruments of his chariots.

And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify himself in his heart, and by peace shall destroy many: he shall also stand up against the Prince of princes; but he shall be broken without hand.
I will also break in pieces with thee the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces captains and rulers.
The weak things of the world shall come forth and break down the mighty and strong ones, that man should not counsel his fellow man, neither trust in the arm of flesh—
Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.
And thus he has laid a cunning plan, thinking to destroy the work of God; but I will require this at their hands, and it shall turn to their shame and condemnation in the day of judgment.
Because they regard not the works of the Lord, nor the operation of his hands, he shall destroy them, and not build them up.
Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

And he made his son pass through the fire, and observed times, and used enchantments, and dealt with familiar spirits and wizards: he wrought much wickedness in the sight of the Lord, to provoke him to anger.
He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and trouble, by sending evil angels among them.
Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?
The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.
Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
Because that for his name’s sake they went forth, taking nothing of the Gentiles.
Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
But there were many among them who would not hear his words; therefore they went their way.
Because they provoked his spirit, so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.
So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.
He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
Then they cry unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saveth them out of their distresses.
Then they cry unto the Lord in their trouble, and he bringeth them out of their distresses.
Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.
That they may offer sacrifices of sweet savours unto the God of heaven, and pray for the life of the king, and of his sons.
When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:
Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?
Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.
Behold, verily I say unto you, there are hypocrites among you, who have deceived some, which has given the adversary power; but behold such shall be reclaimed;

"Behold, I say unto you, there are hypocrites among you, who
have deceived some, which has given the adversary power; but,
behold, such shall be reclaimed; but the hypocrites shall be
detected and cut off, either in life or in death, even as I will;
and woe unto them who are cut off from my Church, for the same are
overcome of the world; wherefore, let every man beware, lest he do
that which is…
That the triumphing of the wicked is short, and the joy of the hypocrite but for a moment?
Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more:

Wherefore, verily I say, let the wicked take heed, and let the rebellious fear and tremble; and let the unbelieving hold their lips, for the day of wrath shall come upon them as a whirlwind, and all flesh shall know that I am God.
I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.
That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.
If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me:
If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
O Lord my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;
That thou inquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?
Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.
Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.

If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?
And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.
These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
And the Lord said unto him: Believest thou the words which I shall speak?
Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
Again I say unto you, hearken and hear and obey the law which I shall give unto you.
Wherefore, hearken and I will reason with you, and I will speak unto you and prophesy, as unto men in days of old.
O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.
Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were without wisdom.
Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.
And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship;
Then said I, Wisdom is better than strength: nevertheless the poor man’s wisdom is despised, and his words are not heard.
He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.
My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.

And devote his whole time to this high and holy calling, which I now give unto him, seeking diligently the kingdom of heaven and its righteousness, and all things necessary shall be added thereunto; for the laborer is worthy of his hire.
Incline not my heart to any evil thing, to practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: and let me not eat of their dainties.
The wicked worketh a deceitful work: but to him that soweth righteousness shall be a sure reward.
For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.

For behold, he has his friends in iniquity, and he keepeth his guards about him; and he teareth up the laws of those who have reigned in righteousness before him; and he trampleth under his feet the commandments of God;

He that walketh righteously, and speaketh uprightly; he that despiseth the gain of oppressions, that shaketh his hands from holding of bribes, that stoppeth his ears from hearing of blood, and shutteth his eyes from seeing evil;

And that he manifesteth himself unto all those who believe in him, by the power of the Holy Ghost; yea, unto every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, working mighty miracles, signs, and wonders, among the children of men according to their faith.
For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.
And gave unto him commandments which inspired him;
